header.subtitle: Local encoding, hashing, encryption and JWT utilities

footer.privacy: All computation stays in your browser; no data leaves the page.

hash.inputPlaceholder: Type text to compute all hashes in real time

rsa.messagePlaceholder: Enter message for encrypt/decrypt/sign; for verify put signature on line 1 and message below

tip.md5: 128-bit hash, broken. Legacy checksums only; never for security.
tip.sha1: 160-bit hash, broken. Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 for new projects.
tip.sha256: 256-bit hash, mainstream. Used in TLS, blockchain, etc.
tip.sha3: Keccak sponge, NIST 2015. Structurally distinct from SHA-2, length-extension resistant.
tip.hmac: Keyed hash that verifies both integrity and authenticity. Only key holders can produce valid HMAC.
tip.aesGcm: AES + GCM mode = AEAD. Built-in auth tag detects tampering. Used in TLS 1.3.
tip.aesCbc: AES + CBC mode, legacy block cipher. No built-in auth; pair with HMAC.
tip.chacha20: Stream cipher, faster than AES without hardware acceleration. RFC 8439.
tip.rsaKeySize: 1024-bit deprecated; 2048-bit minimum; 4096-bit safer but slower. RSA-OAEP for encryption, RSA-PSS for signing — cannot share key pairs.
tip.jwt: JWT = Header.Payload.Signature. Payload is Base64Url-encoded, not encrypted — anyone can read it. Signature guarantees integrity, not confidentiality.
